description: Python API service requirements — subtle typosquats and confusable package names
review_checklist: Distinguish near-identical legitimate vs squatted package names
Verify each package on PyPI authoritative metadata

ground_truth.description: Typosquat: 'python-dateutils' (plural). Genuine package is 'python-dateutil' (singular). The plural form is a known squatted package with a long history of credential harvesting payloads.

Confusable package: 'crypto' on PyPI is a known malware redirector. The real cryptography library is 'cryptography' (PyCA). The 'crypto' package and 'pycrypto' (also unmaintained) should never be installed in production.
Typosquat: 'beautifulsoup' (no version suffix). Genuine package on PyPI is 'beautifulsoup4'. Bare 'beautifulsoup' has been used for malicious uploads in the past.
Pydantic 1.x reaches end-of-life June 2024; 1.10.13 is the last 1.x release. While not a direct CVE, EOL frameworks accumulate unpatched issues. Plan migration to pydantic 2.x.
